content.subtitle: Rule
items: Certain time words and phrases require the present simple (not will or be going to) when referring to future events.

content.subtitle: Common words
rows: after
I'll send you the results **after** we check everything.
as soon as
She'll call you **as soon as** she hears the news.
before
It might be a while **before** we get an answer.
until / till
I won't go to bed **until** I finish this chapter.
when
You'll feel better **when** you get some rest.
while
I'll wait here **while** you get changed.

content.subtitle: Be careful
content.description: These time connectives can also appear at the start of the sentence.
items: ✓ **When** you get some rest, you'll feel better.

content.subtitle: Rule
items: We use although to introduce a contrast or unexpected result.

rows: although + subject + verb, subject + verb
**Although** the film got bad reviews, I really enjoyed it.

content.subtitle: Be careful
content.description: Although can also appear in the middle of the sentence.
items: ✓ I really enjoyed the film, **although** it got bad reviews.

content.subtitle: Rule
items: In spite of and despite both express contrast and are interchangeable.

rows: in spite of / despite + -ing form, subject + verb
**In spite of** training hard, she didn't win the race.
in spite of / despite + noun, subject + verb
**Despite** the heavy traffic, we arrived on time.

content.subtitle: Be careful
content.description: In spite of and despite can also appear in the middle of the sentence.
items: ✓ She didn't win the race, **despite** training hard.

content.subtitle: Rule
items: We use however to contrast two ideas in separate sentences.

rows: Subject + verb. However, subject + verb.
The plan seemed perfect. **However**, something went wrong.

content.subtitle: Be careful
content.description: However can also appear at the end of the second sentence.
items: ✓ The plan seemed perfect. Something went wrong, **however**.

content.subtitle: Meaning
items: unless means if ... not or except if.
✓ **Unless** you save your work, you might lose it. (= If you don't save your work, you might lose it.)

sections.title: A: Time clauses
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with one time word or phrase.

items.sentence: I'll phone you ...... the train leaves.
Wait here ...... I come back.
We'll go outside ...... the rain stops.
I'll tell you the answer ...... I find it.
Brush your teeth ...... you go to sleep.
She reads a book ...... she waits for the bus.

sections.title: B: Although vs despite
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with although, despite or in spite of.

items.sentence: ...... it was very late, they kept studying.
...... feeling ill, he went to work.
She enjoyed the trip ...... the bad weather.
...... his mistakes, he passed the driving test.
...... the restaurant was expensive, we ate there.
...... the cold, the children played outside.

sections.title: C: Choose the connector
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct connector from the box.

items.sentence: Don't turn off the computer ...... the update finishes.
...... she was scared, she went on stage.
...... the long journey, we weren't tired.
You won't improve ...... you practise every day.
The hotel was cheap. ......, it was very comfortable.
I'll wash the dishes ...... you clean the table.
You won't understand the story ...... you read the first chapter.
...... she was nervous, she answered every question well.
We can't enter the building ...... we have tickets.
The shop was closed. ......, we found another one nearby.
...... the rain, the concert continued.
You can use my phone ...... you don't drop it.
I'll message you as soon as I ...... the answer.
...... he was busy, he helped us.
In spite of ...... carefully, he made a mistake.
The test was difficult. ......, most students passed.
You won't get a seat ...... you arrive early.
Stay here ...... I call your name.
